constraints: Total number of cells in one cube doesn't exceed 216
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two integers N and D.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the minimum number of unfriendly people Boris will have to meet.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the minimum number of unfriendly people Boris will have to meet is 1. For the second test case, the minimum number of unfriendly people Boris will have to meet is 5. For the third test case, the minimum number of unfriendly people Boris will have to meet is 1. For the fourth test case, the minimum number of unfriendly people Boris will have to meet is 10.
For the first test case, the minimum number of unfriendly people Boris will have to meet is 0.
For the first test case, the minimum number of unfriendly people Boris will have to meet is 1.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using dynamic programming. We create a DP array dp where dp[i][j][k] represents the minimum number of unfriendly people Boris will have to meet to reach cell (i, j, k). We fill up the DP array by iterating over all cells and for each cell, we calculate the minimum number of unfriendly people by considering all possible previous cells.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N * D^N) per test case, O(T * N * D^N) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N * D^N) — the dominant structure is the DP array.
